items.title: Judge: 1MDB plunder makes Attila the Hun 'look like a choirboy'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The most anticipated full judgment of the 1Malaysia Development Bhd financial scandal involving Datuk Seri Najib Razak was finally out today, six months after he was found guilty of all offences. The 810-page judgment, delivered by presiding judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah, now a Federal Court judge, sets out in detail the court's findings in the globally scaled financial crime. "The scale of the plunder that took place (financially speaking, of course) made Attila the Hun look like a choirboy by comparison," the judge said.

items.title: Malaysia's O&G sector enters two-stage recovery, oil prices support near-term earnings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's oil and gas (O&G) sector is entering a two-stage recovery, with elevated oil prices supporting near-term earnings while a rebuilding of Petroliam Nasional Bhd's (Petronas) capital expenditure (capex) is expected to drive a gradual recovery in job flows, from 2027 onwards, according to AmInvestment Bank Bhd. The investment bank said it expects Petronas' capex to rebuild towards RM45 billion-RM50 billion in the full financial year 2026 to financial year 2028, from RM42 billion in 2025, supported by elevated Brent oil prices and renewed focus on energy security. AmInvestment said Petronas would have stronger operating cash flow to fund reinvestment with Brent oil prices at US$80-90 per barrel.

items.title: Defence Ministry eyes expanded Anka drone fleet for wider surveillance reach
items.summary: LABUAN: The Defence Ministry is looking to acquire additional Anka unmanned aerial systems (UAS) under a second phase procurement to further strengthen the country's intelligence, surveillance and reconnaissance (ISR) capabilities, especially over the South China Sea. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the ministry was planning to acquire another three units, which would double the Royal Malaysian Air Force's drone fleet. He said the additional acquisition is part of efforts to enhance Malaysia's ability to monitor areas of national interest and improve operational coverage nationwide.

items.title: No PN notice received on Hamzah's reappointment as Opposition Leader
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Larut member of Parliament Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin's position as Opposition Leader in the Dewan Rakyat has yet to be officially recognised. Johari said the official decision currently on record still refers to PN's earlier notification naming Kemaman MP Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar, who is also Pas vice-president, as Opposition Leader. "So the earlier official decision from PN still stands (that is) Dr Sam is the Opposition Leader," he said when contacted.

items.title: Gold firms as US-Iran deal cools rate hike bets ahead of Fed meeting
items.summary: NEW YORK: Gold prices firmed on Tuesday after an interim US–Iran deal to end the war in the Persian Gulf eased bets of a US rate hike, with investors awaiting details on the agreement and the Federal Reserve's policy meeting. Spot gold was up 0.8 per cent at US$4,341.39 per ounce at 0851 GMT. US gold futures delivery added 0.3 per cent to US$4,363.00.

items.title: King extends condolences over death of two soldiers in Kedah grenade blast at Hobart Camp
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has conveyed his condolences to the families of two army personnel killed in an explosion during grenade-throwing training at the Hobart Camp shooting range in Gurun, Kedah, this morning. May they be placed among the faithful and the martyrs,” said Sultan Ibrahim, who is also the Supreme Commander of the Malaysian Armed Forces. Earlier, the Malaysian Army confirmed that the victims were Corporal Norazmi Abu Bakar from the 6th Battalion, Royal Malay Regiment (6 RAMD), and Private Siti Khadijah Sungip from the 1st Squadron, Royal Army Engineers Regiment (1 RAJD).

items.title: Malaysia looks to Singapore for housing know-how, says Nga Kor Ming
items.summary: SINGAPORE, June 16 — Malaysia is keen to strengthen cooperation in housing development with Singapore through talent mobility and knowledge-sharing initiatives, Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said. He said that through enhanced cooperation, Malaysia stands to gain valuable insights into its own public housing projects from Singapore’s successful models. “Singapore is well known for its public housing system, specifically the HDB model, as well as its Urban Redevelopment Authority (URA).

items.title: Fahmi: Madani communities must step up to ensure only verified information reaches the public
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 16 — Madani Communities nationwide have been urged to ensure the public receives accurate, verified and reliable information on the government’s policies, initiatives and achievements. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the responsibility should be shared collectively and not left solely to government agencies such as the Information Department (JAPEN) and the Community Communications Department (J-KOM). During the event, Fahmi also presented appointment letters to Kelantan Madani Community leaders for the 2026-2027 term.

items.title: Girl, 15, missing after failing to show up for school in Ampang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are seeking public help to locate a 15-year-old girl who has been missing since yesterday after failing to turn up for school in Pandan Indah, Ampang. Ampang Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Khairul Anuar Khalid said her father lodged a police report after the teen's class teacher informed the family of her absence. Anyone with information on her whereabouts can contact the nearest police station, the Ampang Jaya police headquarters control centre at 03-4289 7222, or investigating officer Inspector Nur Faizah Mohd Anuar at 017-3422804.

items.title: Education Ministry targets over 95pc secondary school enrolment as new law comes into force soon
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 16 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) aims to raise the universal enrolment rate for secondary schools to over 95 per cent, in line with the Education (Amendment) Act 2025, which will come into force soon. Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said the success of primary school enrolment, rising from about 92 per cent in 2003 to over 99 per cent in 2026, proved the effectiveness of the compulsory education policy implemented 23 years ago. Last July, the Dewan Rakyat passed the Education (Amendment) Bill 2025, which expands the definition of compulsory education to include secondary schooling.

items.title: Social networks, online video outweigh traditional media in 2026
items.summary: PARIS: News consumers around the world are now turning more to social media and video platforms than traditional outlets for information, a respected report said Tuesday, warning that old-style business models are under threat. Ffor the first time, social media and video network consumption is now ahead of other news sources as the most widely used source of news globally", at 54 per cent, wrote Jim Egan, lead author of the report from the Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism. This year's edition found 54 per cent of respondents said they got news from social media or video platforms in the week before the survey – rising to 56 per cent if AI chatbots like ChatGPT were included.

items.title: 80,000 patients to benefit from RM5.69mil medicine-by-post programme
items.summary: KOTA BARU: About 80,000 patients suffering from various illnesses nationwide are expected to benefit each month from the government's expanded "medicine by post" programme, which is estimated to cost RM5.69 million annually. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the programme, which will begin simultaneously across all states on July 1, will be fully funded through federal allocations. "The medicines cover all types of prescriptions given to patients of Health Ministry facilities," he said.

items.title: Ignore Anti-Bullying Tribunal award, risk jail and fines
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Individuals who fail to comply with awards issued by the Anti-Bullying Tribunal may face imprisonment and fines under the Anti-Bullying Act 2026. Tribunal president Nurul Husna Awang said Section 44 of the Act provides penalties for perpetrators who disregard tribunal awards. Under Section 44, a person who fails to comply with a tribunal award within 30 days commits an offence.

items.title: Sabah clears January hardcore poverty list, but over 57,000 households remain poor, says Hajiji
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 16 — Sabah has successfully removed all 10,825 household heads classified as hardcore poor at the beginning of this year from the state’s poverty registry, Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said today. Hajiji said the achievement was recorded based on its eKasih data as of May 31, following a series of targeted assistance programmes carried out jointly by the state and federal governments. He said the reduction was achieved through a data-driven approach involving the Sabah State Database System (PADANS), eKasih records and coordinated intervention programmes.

items.title: Zambry plans to include private universities in national service training
items.summary: TANJONG MALIM: The Higher Education Ministry plans to involve private higher education institutions in the National Service Training Programme in the future. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said, however, the matter is subject to discussions with National Service Training Department director-general Maj Gen Datuk Marzuki Mokhtar. "He will determine the sequencing of the programme, including whether private institutions will participate alongside public universities.

items.title: Deadline looms for UniCredit's hostile bid for Commerzbank
items.summary: MILAN: Tuesday marks the final day for UniCredit's hostile and highly political takeover offer for Commerzbank, as Italy's second-largest bank has its sights on an ambitious pan-European banking merger. UniCredit's offer officially ends Tuesday at 11:59 pm (2159 GMT), unless extended. If the offer succeeds and is approved by the European Central Bank, it should mainly allow UniCredit to gradually increase its stake in Commerzbank and merge it with its German subsidiary HypoVereinsbank.

items.title: Penang urges stricter control as refugee numbers top over 20,000
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang government has called for tighter coordination with federal authorities in managing refugees and asylum seekers amid public safety concerns. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said data from the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees as of August 2023 showed that an estimated 20,022 refugees and asylum seekers are currently in Penang. Chow said the state remained committed to a pragmatic but firm approach, stressing that immigration control, border security and refugee status recognition fall under federal jurisdiction.

items.title: MIC to contest four Johor seats in state polls, says party veep Murugiah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — MIC has been allocated four seats in the upcoming Johor state election, said its vice-president Datuk T. Murugiah. He said the matter was confirmed by MIC president Tan Sri SA. “At present, the president has said that we will contest four seats in Johor, while the number of seats MIC will contest in Negeri Sembilan has not yet been finalised.

items.title: KPMG scandal deepens Big Four accounting firms' woes in Australia
items.summary: KPMG's audit-leak scandal is set to further dampen the prospects in Australia of the Big Four accounting firms, with data showing their new business revenue from the federal government already fell by almost half after a similar leak at PwC three years ago. A Reuters analysis of government tenders showed that new contracts the Big Four firms KPMG, PwC, Deloitte and EY signed with the federal Australian government slumped to A$348 million (US$245.51 million) in 2025 from A$637 million in the prior year. The Australian government said on Tuesday KPMG will not bid for new federal work until September 30.

items.title: RTD cracks down on foreign motorists in Puchong
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Road Transport Department seized 34 vehicles and issued 336 notices during a special enforcement operation targeting foreign motorists in Bandar Puteri Puchong today. He said enforcement personnel took action against 34 vehicles and recorded 218 offences involving road tax, insurance, licence and other technical violations. Among the offences were 56 cases each involving expired road tax and insurance coverage, 50 cases involving driving licence offences, two vocational licence offences, two cases of road tax misuse, 29 technical offences and 23 other offences.

items.title: Sabah welcomes Japan’s decision to ease travel advisory for parts of east coast as security improves
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 16 — Sabah has welcomed Japan’s decision to downgrade its travel advisory for parts of the state’s east coast, describing the move as international recognition of improving security conditions and growing confidence in the state’s tourism industry. State Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said the revised advisory by Japan’s Foreign Ministry reflected years of efforts by Malaysian security agencies to strengthen enforcement, monitoring and coordination in eastern Sabah. Sabah minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said Japan’s decision to ease its travel advisory for eastern Sabah reflected improved security and stronger enforcement in the area.

items.title: BN unfazed by emergence of new parties ahead of Johor, N. Sembilan polls, says its sec-gen
items.summary: TANJUNG MALIM, June 16 — The formation of new political parties in the country will not affect Barisan Nasional's (BN) prospects in the upcoming Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections, said BN secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir. “Johor Umno and BN have prepared extensively, and I believe the formation of a new party will have no impact on BN, based on the efforts and preparations that have already been set in motion,” he said. He said this when asked whether the emergence of Parti Wawasan Negara and Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) would pose a challenge to BN in the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections.

items.title: End to US-Iran conflict a relief for markets, says Penang Chief Minister
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The expected end of the United States-Iran conflict this week is welcome news for the global economy, Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said. Chow said the conflict had caused significant disruptions worldwide, affecting energy supplies, logistics, commodity prices and global supply chains. Chow said Malaysia had taken proactive measures to mitigate the impact of the conflict, including maintaining energy subsidies and monitoring inflationary pressures.

items.title: Stocks extend rally, oil falls further as peace optimism builds
items.summary: HONG KONG: Equities extended their rally and oil prices fell further on Tuesday, fuelled by the US-Iran peace deal and as tech firms tracked another blockbuster performance by SpaceX. US President Donald Trump said ships were again moving through the Strait of Hormuz and it would be "completely open" by Friday, while Iranian media said three oil tankers and two cargo ships had passed through the vital waterway that had been subject to a US naval blockade. However, a senior US administration official said Trump, Vice President JD Vance and Iran's parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf had already signed the text electronically.

items.title: 1MDB among world's biggest kleptocracy scandals, court says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court has described the 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) scandal as a financial plunder of such staggering proportions that it made the notorious conqueror Attila the Hun look "like a choirboy by comparison." Presiding judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah, in his 810-page grounds of judgment, underscored the unprecedented scale of the scandal and the extraordinary length of the trial. "The scale of the plunder that took place (financially speaking, of course) made Attila the Hun look like a choirboy by comparison," the judge said.

items.title: Selangor JPJ, MBSJ target cargo bicycles used by foreign nationals in Puchong road safety operation
items.summary: PUCHONG, June 16 — The Selangor Road Transport Department (JPJ), in collaboration with the Subang Jaya City Council (MBSJ), conducted an integrated Foreign Driver Operation (Ops PeWA) in Puchong today to detect the use of cargo bicycles by foreign nationals, which are alleged to pose a risk to road users. The operation was carried out following complaints from residents about the use of cargo bicycles by foreign nationals on main roads, including claims that they were using busy routes without regard for the safety of other road users. Among the concerns raised were the use of these poorly maintained bicycles on high-traffic routes and the act of ferrying children in the cargo compartment without safety features.

items.title: Austrian electronics firm AT&S to pump US$2.3bil into Kulim plant
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the expansion was finalised following discussions in early June, adding that it would strengthen Malaysia's semiconductor ecosystem and create high-skilled jobs for locals. "The confidence shown by international investors like AT&S proves that clear policies, political stability, good governance, and the government's commitment to driving economic transformation are bearing fruit," Anwar said in a Facebook post today. He added that the move places Malaysia on the right track as a premier destination for advanced technology investments and a new economic growth hub in the region.

items.title: India limits cough syrup sales to pharmacies after contamination cases
items.summary: NEW DELHI: India has restricted cough syrup sales to licensed pharmacies especially in villages, preventing over-the-counter sales from retail outlets, as regulators step up scrutiny following contamination-linked child deaths last year. Some cough syrups were previously on an exemption list under drug rules, allowing them to be sold over the counter as household remedies under relaxed regulations. In cities, however, existing rules already require cough syrups to be sold only in registered pharmacies, a health ministry official said on condition of anonymity.

items.title: China expands higher education and vocational ties with Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: China is set to offer more academic opportunities and enhanced support services for Malaysian students to pursue higher education in the country, said Chinese Ambassador to Malaysia Ouyang Yujing. "In the field of education, China and Malaysia have already carried out extensive cooperation through teacher and student exchanges, short-term study programs, joint training, and collaborative education. Ouyang said both nations have also strengthened vocational education ties by pioneering the "Chinese language plus vocational skills" training model through school-enterprise collaborations.

items.title: Hydroshoppe's KL Tower bribery trial to start on Jan 4 next year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court today fixed Jan 4 next year as the start date for the trial of Hydroshoppe Sdn Bhd and its directors over the alleged offer of RM500,000 a year for 15 years to Tan Sri Annuar Musa. Judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin set the dates during case management proceedings attended by Deputy Public Prosecutor Ifa Sirrhu Samsudin, from the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC), and lawyer Tang Jia Yearn, who appeared for Hydroshoppe Sdn Bhd director Datuk Abdul Hamid Shaikh Abdul Razak Shaikh. The court fixed the trial dates for Jan 4 to 7, Feb 2 to 4, March 16 to 18, June 8 to 10, and June 14 to 17 and June 21 to 23, 2027.

items.title: From shattered elbow to recovery, security guard credits Perkeso rehabilitation centre
items.summary: IPOH: A motorcycle crash that left him unable to move his arm has become a defining test of resilience for Universiti Tunku Abdul Rahman (UTAR) security guard Omar Abdul Hamid. The father of four was among the earliest patients at the Sultan Nazrin Shah Rehabilitation Centre in Meru, where he began a structured rehabilitation programme on July 22, 2025, during the facility's early operations. Sultan of Perak Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah officiated the opening ceremony of the centre.

items.title: SoftBank Vision Fund CFO is leaving company, memo shows
items.summary: TOKYO: The chief financial officer (CFO) of SoftBank Group's Vision Fund investing arm, Navneet Govil, is leaving the company after a decade there, according to an internal memo reviewed by Reuters. SoftBank Vision Fund declined to comment. Govil, CFO of SoftBank Investment Advisors, first joined SoftBank in 2016, a year before the launch of the first Vision Fund, which upended the startup investing world with its high-conviction bets.

items.title: US military to build war-ready stockpile in Australia
items.summary: SYDNEY: The US military is planning a permanent war-ready weapons stockpile for its Marine Corps on Australia's southeast coast beyond the range of most Chinese missiles, tender documents show and officials confirmed to AFP. Documents published by the US Navy this month show advanced planning for an even larger Australian stockpile, with US$30 million allocated to build warehouses and offices in southeastern Victoria state for "critical forward provisioning." "Marine Corps activities in Australia support integrated global sustainment by maintaining ready-for-issue equipment and supplies for operations and exercises across the Indo-Pacific," a US Marine Corps Forces Pacific spokesperson told AFP.

items.title: Malaysia's SME Sentiment Index drops to record low of 45.1 in 1H 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The SME Sentiment Index (SSI) fell to a record low of 45.1 in the first half of 2026 (1H 2026), down from 55.6 in 2H 2025, according to Small Medium Enterprise Development Bank Malaysia Bhd (SME Bank). The bank said that although economic sentiment among MSMEs has deteriorated to a record low, their operational outlook remains comparatively resilient. SME Bank said that despite softer growth expectations, most MSMEs still anticipate higher sales, although business confidence has weakened considerably.

items.title: Is your child bullying others? New law makes parents legally responsible, says Azalina
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — Parents may now be held legally liable for acts of bullying committed by their children under the Anti-Bullying Act 2026, which introduces the concept of shared family responsibility. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the provision is among the key reforms under the Act, extending accountability for bullying misconduct beyond the perpetrator to family members. In contrast, the Anti-Bullying Act incorporates an element of shared responsibility that may bind family members, including in relation to the payment of fines imposed.

items.title: Hardships shaped Fuad Stephens' life, taught him empathy, says daughter
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The hardships and low points in Sabah's first chief minister Tun Fuad Stephens' life helped shape his character and taught him empathy, his daughter Fauziah said. Fuad, born Donald Aloysius Marmaduke Stephens, struggled to make ends meet in Singapore following the Japanese invasion, at one point collecting and burying bodies. We were never short of guests," said Fauziah, the Pitas-born adopted daughter of the former chief minister.

items.title: Penang to work closely with Putrajaya on refugee and asylum seeker management, says CM
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 16 — The Penang state government is fully supportive of more systematic and controlled management of refugees in the state, particularly the Refugee Registration Document (DPP) mechanism, said Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow. “For the state government, our priority is to ensure public safety, the well-being of local communities and compliance with the law are always preserved,” he said in a statement issued today. “The Penang state government has always adopted a pragmatic, prudent and responsible approach in dealing with the issue of refugees and asylum seekers in the state, in line with the policies and decisions set by the federal government,” he said.

items.title: Medicine stocks stable, only 14 items deemed critical
items.summary: Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the ministry tracks 702 medicine types covering 3,739 registered products through monthly reports submitted by registration holders. "As of May 8, 2026, 505 medicine types, or 72 per cent, had stock levels exceeding 90 days and were classified as low risk. However, Dzulkefly said checks with ministry facilities and concession companies found that 13 of the 14 critical medicines still have stock levels exceeding 90 days.

items.title: Woman charged with neglect after son dies, daughter injured in fall from apartment
items.summary: Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the 37-year-old suspect, who is the mother of the two children, was charged under Section 31(1)(a) of the Child Act 2001 for child abuse, neglect, abandonment, or exposure. He added that the suspect was arrested by a team from the Johor Baru Utara district police criminal investigation department on June 7. The suspect is currently remanded in prison pending the next case mention on July 22.f the investigation paper, which was referred to the Johor prosecution director's office before charging instructions were issued by the deputy public prosecutor.

items.title: RNG Tech targets RM16.4mil IPO to fund regional expansion plans
items.summary: The IPO involves the issuance of 126.1 million new shares, representing 16 per cent of the enlarged share capital, as well as an offer for sale of 78.8 million existing shares. Meanwhile, of the 78.8 million existing shares offered for sale, 23.6 million shares will be placed with selected Bumiputera investors approved by the ministry, while the remaining 55.2 million shares will be allocated to selected investors through private placement. Another RM1 million will be spent on marketing activities, RM3 million on repaying borrowings, RM1.1 million on working capital, and RM4.2 million for listing expenses.

items.title: BN to finalise Johor, N. Sembilan seat allocation next week, says Umno sec-gen
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — Negotiations on seat allocation for Barisan Nasional (BN) in the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections are expected to be concluded next week, said Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki. The Election Commission (EC) has set July 11 as polling day for the Johor state election, with June 27 as nomination day and July 7 for early voting. For the Negeri Sembilan state election, the EC has set August 1 as polling day, while July 18 is nomination day and July 28 for early voting.

items.title: Pioneer psychiatrist Dr M. Mahadevan dies at 96
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Dr M. Mahadevan, widely regarded as the father of modern psychiatry in Malaysia and the founding president of the Malaysian Psychiatric Association, has died at 96. Mahadevan, who also served as the government chief psychiatrist, was a pioneering figure in the development of mental healthcare services in the country. Members of the public may pay their last respects at 32, Lorong Lee Hin Neo 1, Ukay Heights, Kuala Lumpur, from 10am to 1pm and 3pm to 6pm today and tomorrow.

items.title: Johor MB calls on Putrajaya to fast-track JB’s rapid transit project ahead of RTS Link
items.summary: KULAI, June 16 — Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has urged the federal government to expedite the city centre’s rapid transit project to relieve traffic in the city centre once the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link begins operations on January 1 next year. Onn Hafiz said that the city centre’s rapid transit project project had already been tabled in the Cabinet meeting back in 2024. However, he said the physical work for the project has not yet commenced.

items.title: National Service training programme to be expanded to private universities, says higher education minister
items.summary: TANJONG MALIM, June 16 — The Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE) is planning to involve private higher education institutions (IPTS) in the National Service Training Programme (PLKN) in the future. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said, however, that the matter is subject to discussions with National Service Training Department (JLKN) director-general Maj Gen Datuk Marzuki Mokhtar. “We will also look at state-owned private higher education institutions, as every state has its own universities.

items.title: Chow Kon Yeow: Penang-Perak water deal talks enter final stretch, signing targeted for next week
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 16 — The long-awaited Perak-Penang water transfer project is now in its final stages of negotiations, with a formal agreement expected to be signed as early as next week, Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today. He said both state governments have tentatively agreed to conclude and sign the deal by the end of next week in Ipoh, Perak. Meanwhile, Chow said the state government would announce its decision after receiving feedback from the concessionaire of the proposed Jelutong landfill rehabilitation and reclamation project along Karpal Singh Drive.

items.title: Penang orders closure of 177 food premises over hygiene violations
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 16 — A total of 177 food premises in Penang have been ordered to temporarily close for various hygiene violations following inspections of 4,069 premises by the Penang Health Department (JKNPP) from January to April this year. State Youth, Sports and Health Committee chairman Gooi Zi Sen said the enforcement action involved various categories of premises, including restaurants, eateries, school canteens, hospital cafeterias, and factory canteens, which were found to have failed to comply with established hygiene and food safety standards, based on operations and public complaints. Gooi said JKNPP also received 241 complaints related to food premises this year, with 185 complaints involving the cleanliness of restaurants and eateries, 12 involving supermarkets, nine involving stalls or hawker stands, and eight involving food courts.

items.title: Medicine supply stable in Malaysia despite monitoring of select drugs, says Health Ministry
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 16 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has assured that medicine supplies in Malaysia remain stable and sufficient, although a small number of products continue to be closely monitored. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the ministry tracks 702 medicine types covering 3,739 registered products through monthly reports submitted by registration holders. “As of May 8, 2026, 505 medicine types, or 72 per cent, had stock levels exceeding 90 days and were classified as low risk.

items.title: Kedah animal feed factory raided over suspected illegal flour storage
items.summary: BALING, June 16 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) Kedah branch raided an animal feed processing factory in the Kuala Ketil Industrial Area yesterday over the suspected storage of wheat flour without proper authorisation. Kedah KPDN director Muhammad Nizam Jamaludin said four enforcement personnel from the Baling branch carried out the operation at about 4.30pm. “The inspection uncovered wheat flour believed to have been used in the company’s animal feed processing activities,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: ‘Same table’ after all? Loke, Onn Hafiz meet in Kulai after public spat over Johor polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke Siew Fook and Johor caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi reportedly met today at the launch of the Shuttle Selatan service in Kulai, after days of public exchanges over political cooperation and federal-state ties. Sinar Harian reported that Loke, who is also DAP secretary-general, arrived at the Kulai Railway Station event at 12.45pm, while Onn Hafiz, the Johor Barisan Nasional chairman, arrived about five minutes later. Also present was Johor Public Works, Transport, Infrastructure and Communications Committee chairman Mohamad Fazli Mohamad Salleh.

items.title: Ikram official pleads not guilty to 158 graft charges involving RM98m
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 16 — An official of Pertubuhan Ikram Malaysia (Ikram) pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court today to 158 charges of using his position for gratification totalling more than RM98 million between 2021 and 2025. Fakhrudin Abd Karim, 57, entered the plea after all charges were read out during proceedings that lasted nearly two hours before Judge Datuk Mohd Nasir Nordin. Under the first 149 charges, Fakhrudin was accused, in his capacity as an authorised signatory to a Maybank Islamic account belonging to Ikram, of using his position to obtain RM81.95 million in bribes for an associate, namely a non-governmental organisation (NGO).

items.title: Federal Court awards RM20,000 each to seven Orang Asli villagers over desecrated ancestral graves
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 16 — The Federal Court today ruled in favour of seven Orang Asli villagers by ordering that each receive RM20,000 in general damages following the destruction of their ancestral graves. A three-member bench comprising Justices Datuk Nordin Hassan, Datuk Seri Vazeer Alam Mydin Meera and Datuk Lee Swee Seng unanimously allowed the villagers’ appeal and reinstated the earlier decision of the Kuantan High Court. The ruling overturned the Court of Appeal’s October 2024 decision, which had favoured Agrobest (M) Sdn Bhd, a company involved in the prawn farming industry, and two of its employees.

items.title: Marang council says Rohingya learning centre at Telaga Mengkudu legally leased since 2020
items.summary: MARANG, June 16 — The Marang District Council (MDM) has clarified that premises at the Telaga Mengkudu Complex in Bukit Payong, which recently went viral after being alleged to operate as a learning centre for Rohingya children, have been legally leased to a non-governmental organisation (NGO) supported by the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) since 2020. In a statement today, MDM said the premises were rented to Persatuan Jaringan Islam Global Masa Depan in accordance with established procedures, strict conditions and regulations for educational and humanitarian welfare activities. The council said it had not received any official complaints throughout the tenancy period regarding breaches of rental conditions that could affect public safety, public order or community harmony.

items.title: Two soldiers killed in grenade explosion during training exercise in Kedah
items.summary: GURUN, June 16 — Two military personnel are believed to have died following a grenade explosion during a training exercise at the Hobart Camp Shooting Range here today. Kuala Muda police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan, when contacted, confirmed the incident and said police received information regarding the case at about 12.30pm today. Meanwhile, the Ministry of Defence is expected to issue an official statement regarding the incident soon.

items.title: Silat trainee pleads not guilty in KL to sedition over Facebook posts insulting Yang di-Pertuan Agong institution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — A silat trainee pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to publishing seditious posts and insulting the institution of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong last year. Deputy Public Prosecutor Nur Shakila Mohd Shariff proposed bail at RM8,000 with one surety, and an additional condition that the accused’s passport be surrendered to the court pending disposal of the case. Judge Tasnim Abu Bakar allowed the accused bail of RM5,000 with one surety, with the additional condition that his passport be surrendered to the court, and fixed July 13 for mention of the case.

items.title: Socso claims could jump 200pc a year with new protection schemes, says HR minister
items.summary: “Through the development of this centre (the Sultan Nazrin Shah Perkeso Rehabilitation Centre), we aim to transform the country’s rehabilitation landscape in tandem with technological advancements and the growing need for a more dynamic rehabilitation approach,” he said in his speech at the opening of the Sultan Nazrin Shah Perkeso Rehabilitation Centre in Meru Raya here today. The Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah, opened the centre, which was previously known as the Perkeso Neuro-Robotic Rehabilitation and Cybernics Centre. On the Sultan Nazrin Shah Perkeso Rehabilitation Centre, he said the facility, with a treatment capacity of up to 700 patients at any one time, is capable of benefiting 3,000 patients annually.

items.title: PKR says it will contest Puteri Wangsa in Johor election, seat previously ceded to Muda
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — PKR is reportedly set to contest Puteri Wangsa in the coming Johor election, taking over a state seat that was given to Muda under its previous electoral arrangement with Pakatan Harapan (PH) in 2022. Free Malaysia Today reported that Johor PKR vice-chairman Jimmy Puah said the arrangement had been agreed among PH parties, including Amanah, which had also been eyeing the constituency. “I can confirm that PKR will contest Puteri Wangsa.

items.title: Transport Ministry, JPJ target July completion for MyLesen B2 rollout nationwide, says Anthony Loke
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, June 16 — The Ministry of Transport (MOT) and the Road Transport Department (JPJ) are targeting completion of the MyLesen B2 programme by July to help more youths obtain valid motorcycle licences. “We hope it will help more youths comply with existing laws when driving or riding motorcycles,” he said at the Youth Empowerment Programme, MyLesen B2 and MyLesen B2 Sekolah (MyB2S) event at SJK(C) Yong Peng 1 today. Loke said the MyB2S programme, implemented with the Ministry of Education, involves 60,000 students nationwide with an allocation of RM18.5 million.

items.title: Home Ministry denies claims of neglect of Rohingya children, says Baitul Mahabbah protects migrant, refugee kids
items.summary: The Home Ministry (KDN) said claims that children are detained without protection or denied basic welfare do not reflect the actual efforts being undertaken to manage irregular migration responsibly, humanely and in line with national security requirements. “As of February 28, 2026, a total of six Baitul Mahabbah shelters were operating nationwide, accommodating 188 children, including seven classified as unaccompanied or separated children,” the statement said. “The government, through the Home Ministry and the Immigration Department, has implemented various measures to ensure that the welfare and protection of the children involved continue to receive due attention,” the ministry said in a statement today.

items.title: Six firms probed over alleged RM5.7m AADK tender cartel, says MyCC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — The Malaysian Competition Commission (MyCC) has issued a Proposed Decision against six companies over suspected involvement in a bid-rigging cartel linked to a RM5.7 million food supply tender under the National Anti-Drug Agency (AADK). MyCC chairman Tan Sri Idrus Harun said investigations conducted by the commission found indications that the companies had engaged in information sharing and collusion during the preparation and submission of their tender bids. “These companies were found to have successively breached Section 4(1), read together with Sections 4(2)(d) and 4(3) of the Competition Act 2010 [Act 712].

items.title: Austrian chipmaker AT&S to invest up to RM9.4b more in Kulim, boosting Malaysia’s semiconductor ambitions, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — Technologie & Systemtechnik Aktiengesellschaft’s (AT&S) decision to expand its operations in Kulim, Kedah, through a new investment of up to €2 billion (RM9.4 billion) is expected to further strengthen Malaysia’s semiconductor ecosystem and create high-skilled employment opportunities for Malaysians, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said the Austrian company’s decision reflected growing international investor confidence in Malaysia, underscoring how clear policies, political stability, good governance and the government’s commitment to economic transformation are positioning the country as a leading destination for advanced technology investments and a new regional growth hub. AT&S chief financial officer Gerrit Steen said the €1.5 billion to €2.0 billion investment was fully supported and financed through long-term customer commitments.
